items.text: Elevator in Hadley Hall will be out of service from May 18 through May 22 for inspection. Residents on floors 4 through 7 may use the freight lift at the rear entrance with a $5 access pass from the Facilities Office in Room 102.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where can residents obtain the freight-lift pass?

options.C: From the Facilities Office in Room 102

questions.explanation: The notice states that the pass is available from the Facilities Office in Room 102.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents on the lower floors during the outage?

options.A: They will likely use the regular stairs as usual.
options.B: They are required to register for an access pass.
options.C: They must relocate to another residence hall.
options.D: They will receive a refund of their housing fees.

questions.explanation: Only floors 4 through 7 are offered the freight lift, suggesting lower floors continue normal stair use.

items.text: Hi Daniel, Our records show a balance of $42.75 on your spring meal plan. You can apply this credit to next semester or request a refund by June 15. Stop by the Dining Services Office in Whitmore Hall to choose. Thanks, Carla Reyes, Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the deadline for requesting a refund?
questions.explanation: The email specifies that refunds may be requested by June 15.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Daniel's meal plan?

options.A: He has chosen the rollover option already.
options.B: He must visit the office to confirm his enrollment.
options.C: He never used his account during the spring term.
options.D: He has more credit remaining than he spent recently.

questions.explanation: The $42.75 balance implies leftover credit, suggesting Daniel spent less than was loaded.

items.text: Just tried the new lavender honey latte at Birch Cafe — seriously dreamy! Only $4.50 with a student ID and they're brewing it through Friday. Perfect study fuel before finals. Anyone else hooked yet?

questions.stem: According to the post, what is special about the new drink?

options.A: It includes a discount for students with ID.
options.B: It is served only in the afternoon hours.
options.C: It contains a brand-new flavor of espresso.
options.D: It is offered exclusively to club members.

questions.explanation: The post mentions that the latte is $4.50 with a student ID.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the drink's availability?

options.A: It will become part of the regular menu soon.
options.B: It will be removed from the menu after Friday.
options.C: It is only sold during morning hours.
options.D: It requires a reservation to order.

questions.explanation: The post says the cafe is brewing it through Friday, implying it ends afterward.

items.text: South Lot B will be closed for repaving from June 3 through June 7. Permit holders may park in North Lot D at no additional cost during this period; visitors must pay the regular daily rate of $5 at the gate.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must visitors pay during the closure?
questions.explanation: The notice states that visitors must pay the regular daily rate of $5.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about permit holders during the repaving?

options.A: They will lose access to their parking benefits.
options.B: They will receive a refund for unused permit days.
options.C: They will not have to pay any extra fees for relocation.
options.D: They will need to apply for a new permit by mail.

questions.explanation: Permit holders may park in North Lot D at no additional cost during the closure.
